Title: Freeze-Frame Hero: Peak-Action Athlete At The Decisive Millisecond
Teaser: Captures the single frozen instant a play is won — sweat droplets suspended mid-air, muscles taut, the ball or body locked tack-sharp by a high shutter speed — shot like a wire-service photographer who waited all game for this one frame.
PromptHint: Name the [SPORT/ATHLETE] and the exact [PEAK MOMENT] you want frozen (e.g. the foot striking the ball, the dunk at apex).
Prompt: ROLE: You are a wire-service sports photographer who shoots the decisive instant of a play, writing one image prompt for a GPT-4o-style generator with a Midjourney fallback line. TASK: produce a single photoreal freeze-frame of [SPORT/ATHLETE] at this exact peak moment: [PEAK MOMENT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): pick the millisecond just before resolution, where the body is most loaded and the eyes most intense, and decide what tiny detail (a sweat droplet, a chalk puff, a divot of turf) proves the speed. THEN GENERATE: the athlete frozen mid-motion with every muscle defined, gritted determination on the face, beads of sweat suspended in air, fabric and hair caught mid-snap; light it like a stadium with hard directional key from above plus a cool rim light separating the body from a darkened, depth-of-field-blurred crowd; shoot it tight and low for power. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal, anatomically correct (five fingers, natural joints), no warped equipment, no readable sponsor logos or text, exactly one hero athlete sharp while everything else falls to bokeh. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished image, then on a new line a Midjourney param line: '--ar 3:2 --v 6 --style raw --s 250', then one sentence I can paste back to swap the sport or the frozen moment.

PromptHint: Name the fast-moving [SUBJECT] and the [SETTING] that should blur into streaks behind it.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a motorsport and track photographer who lives for the panning shot, writing one image prompt for a GPT-4o-style generator with a Midjourney fallback line. TASK: produce a single photoreal speed-pan of [SUBJECT] tearing through [SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the direction of travel, then commit to the panning trick — the camera tracks the subject so it stays sharp while everything else smears horizontally. THEN GENERATE: the [SUBJECT] crisp and in-focus, frozen mid-effort, while the background and foreground melt into long horizontal motion-blur streaks that scream velocity; add a little blur to the spinning wheels or pumping limbs to sell the speed, with low afternoon light raking across for glint and contrast. CONSTRAINTS: photoreal, the subject must stay tack-sharp against a fully streaked background, keep proportions correct, no floating limbs, no text or logos, one subject. OUTPUT FORMAT: the finished image, then on a new line a Midjourney param line: '--ar 16:9 --v 6 --style raw --s 200', then one sentence I can paste back to push the blur harder or change the direction of travel.
